div1.entryFree:
Demeanour, 1) motion expressive of sentiment, external deportment, gesture: which he by dumb d. seeks to show, Lucr. 474. with such a deep d. in great sorrow, H4B IV, 5, 85 (i. e. a demeanour deep in sorrow, or of deep sorrow). I perceive but cold d. in Octavius' wing, Caes. V, 2, 4.
2) behaviour: fashion your d. to my looks, Err. II, 2, 33. ignoble in d. H6B III, 2, 210.
